root_text.an4.262:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu nālaṁ araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevituṁ.
root_text.an4.262:1.3: Kāmavitakkena, byāpādavitakkena, vihiṁsāvitakkena, duppañño hoti jaḷo elamūgo—
root_text.an4.262:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu nālaṁ araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevituṁ.
root_text.an4.262:2.1: Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevituṁ.
root_text.an4.262:2.3: Nekkhammavitakkena, abyāpādavitakkena, avihiṁsāvitakkena, paññavā hoti ajaḷo anelamūgo—
root_text.an4.262:2.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevitun”ti.

translation_text.an4.262:1.1: “Mendicants, when a mendicant has four qualities they’re not ready to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an4.262:1.3: They have sensual, malicious, and cruel thoughts; or they’re witless, dull, and idiotic.
translation_text.an4.262:1.4: When a mendicant possesses these four qualities they’re not ready to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an4.262:2.1: When a mendicant has four qualities they’re ready to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an4.262:2.3: They have thoughts of renunciation, good will, and harmlessness; and they’re wise, bright, and clever.
translation_text.an4.262:2.4: When a mendicant possesses these four qualities they’re ready to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.”
